title: Contributors
text: CONTRIBUTORS Allowah has been exploring consciousness for a long time. In his early teens he took to running and philosophy, reading voraciously/neurotically, and disdaining most other human company, culminating in what in retrospect might have been a kundalini awakening at nineteen that left him in bliss for months. That was his initiation into actual experience of the deeper dimensions of existence/consciousness. He is a teacher at the Sunshine State Yoga Academy in Naples, Florida. Researcher and author Chris Carter received his undergraduate and master’s degrees from the University of Oxford. The author of Parapsychology and the Skeptics and Science and the Near Death Experience , Carter is originally from Canada and currently teaches internationally. Erik Davis is a writer, award-winning journalist, and teacher based in San Francisco. He is the author, most recently, of Nomad Codes: Adventures in Modern Esoterica . He also wrote The Visionary State: A Journey through California’s Spiritual Landscape with photographs by Michael Rauner, as well as the books Led Zeppelin IV and Tech-Gnosis: Myth, Magic, and Mysticism in the Age of Information , which has been translated into five languages. His essays on art, music, technoculture, and contemporary spirituality have appeared in well over a dozen books, and Davis has contributed articles and essays to a variety of publications, including Bookforum, ArtForum, Salon, Blender , the LA Weekly , and the Village Voice . He hosts the podcast Expanding Mind on the Progressive Radio Network and is currently pursuing a PhD at Rice University. Paul Devereux is an experienced and respected author and researcher primarily dealing with archaeological themes and ancient lifeways, unusual geophysical phenomena, and consciousness studies. While making his subject matter attractive and accessible to a wide audience, his material is factually based. He has written or co-written twenty-five books since 1979. He has also written a range of peer-reviewed academic papers. Based in the Cotswolds, England, he is a research fellow in the International Consciousness Research Laboratories (ICRL) group in Princeton; a full member of the Society for Scientific Exploration (USA); a member of the Scientific and Medical Network; a member of the Folklore Society; a member of the Traditional Cosmology Society; a member of the Society for Psychical Research; and a member of the Society for the Anthropology of Consciousness (USA). Jennifer Dumpert is a San Francisco–based writer and lecturer and the founder and leader of the experiential dream group Oneironauticum. She has taught classes on dreams as adjunct faculty at Pacifica Graduate Institute. Some of her lectures on dreams can be viewed at www.urbandreamscape.com . She posts daily to Twitter: @OneiroFer. Conner Habib is a writer, gay porn star, and teacher. He’s pretty sure he’s the only person who’s won awards for all three of those things. He studied creative writing as well as organismic and evolutionary biology at the University of Massachusetts. He lives in San Francisco, where he spends most of his time reading, writing, and running a Rudolf Steiner and anthroposophy discussion group. His blog is www.connerhabib.wordpress.com , and you can also follow him on Twitter (@ConnerHabib) if you’re eighteen or older. His adult site (18+ only, contains adult content) is www.connerhabib.com . Paul Hughes is the executive director of Vivation International. He is an internationally recognized speaker, empowerment teacher, firewalking instructor, futurist, systems thinker, writer, visionary artist, juggler and freelance human being. Paul has dedicated his life to the service and uplifting of humanity. You can find all of Paul’s past and present writings at www.enthea.org . Paul lives near Lake Tahoe, Nevada. Ryan Hurd is an independent consciousness researcher located in Philadelphia, PA. His work centers around re-invoking the imaginal realms in a post-rational age. Author of The Lucid Immersion Blueprint and Sleep Paralysis , Ryan is editor of www.DreamStudies.org and faculty at www.TheDreamTribe.com . He is also a board member of the International Association for the Study of Dreams and a member of the Society for the Anthropology of Consciousness. Franklin LaVoie is a visionary artist, with a background in earth science education. Franklin underwent intense shamanic initiations throughout the 1970s to become a natural healer. His research appears in a wide variety of books, including, most recently: The 2012 Story , by John Major Jenkins (2009); Shadows of the Western Door , by Mason Winfield (1997); and Planet Heartworks , by Daniel Winter and Friends (1989). Franklin’s essay “Why Nature and The Arts Can Heal Us” (2007) was included in The Society for the Arts in Healthcare’s international conference in 2009. Franklin designs stage sets, mechanical contraptions, and musical instruments, and he’s written plays, short stories, and poetry. He is preparing to publish The Sacred Geography of Buffalo-Niagara . A pioneer in the field of spiritual emergence, in 1981 Paul Levy was drafted into a higher-dimensional shamanic initiation process which mimicked psychosis but was an experience of a far different order. A healer, Paul is in private practice helping others who are also spiritually awakening. As the ex–bookstore manager of the C. G. Jung Foundation of New York, he has been studying Jung’s work for close to thirty years. An artist, Paul is a long-time Tibetan Buddhist practitioner. He is the author of The Madness of George W. Bush: A Reflection of Our Collective Psychosis and Wetiko: The Greatest Epidemic Sickness Known to Humanity (both of which can be ordered on his website, www.awakeninthedream.com ). Paul has developed and facilitates “Awakening in the Dream” groups to help awaken participants to the dreamlike nature of reality. David Luke is senior lecturer in psychology at the University of Greenwich, UK, where he teaches an undergraduate course on the psychology of exceptional human experiences, focusing on his two main interests of psychedelics and the paranormal. He is also a guest lecturer at the University of Northampton, UK, for the MSc in transpersonal psychology and consciousness studies, and is past president of the Parapsychological Association. Since 2008 he has directed the Ecology, Cosmos and Consciousness lecture series at the October Gallery in Bloomsbury, London, and he is one of the core organizers of Breaking Convention: A Multidisciplinary Meeting on Psychedelic Consciousness. He lives life on the edge, of Hackney. David Metcalfe is books editor for The Revealer , the online journal of NYU’s Center for Religion and Media. An independent researcher and artist focusing on the interstices of art, culture, and consciousness, his essay “Of Dice and Divinity—Some Thoughts on Gambling and the Western Tradition,” was included in the 2011 publication of The Immanence of Myth , from Weaponized Press. Writing and scrawling regularly for The Eyeless Owl , his illustrations were brought to life in the animated collaborative grotesquery A Serious Enquiry Into the Vulgar Notion of Nature , featured at select venues in downtown Chicago during the spring and fall of 2010. He contributes regularly to Evolutionary Landscapes, Alarm Magazine , Reality Sandwich, and The Revealer , and is currently co-hosting the Art of Transformations study group, dedicated to a contemporary study of alchemy and Hermetica, with support from the International Alchemy Guild. Jay Michaelson is the author of four books and two hundred articles on the intersections of religion, spirituality, sexuality, and law. His most recent book, God vs. Gay? The Religious Case for Equality (Beacon), was an Amazon.com bestseller and has been nominated for a 2012 Lambda Literary Award. Jay’s work has appeared in Salon, Newsweek, Tikkun, The Huffington Post , and other publications. His other books include Everything is God: The Radical Path of Nondual Judaism and Another Word for Sky: Poems . Jay’s advocacy on behalf of sexual minorities in religious communities has been featured on CNN, NPR, and in the New York Times . He received his JD from Yale Law School and will soon be completing his PhD in Jewish thought at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem. Jay has held teaching positions at Boston University Law School, City College of New York, and Yale University. Journalist, photographer, and artist James Oroc was born in the small South Pacific nation of Aotearoa. Since 1998 he has been pursuing and reporting on the cutting edge of extreme sports in more than forty countries around the globe, his work appearing in magazines, films, and on MTV Sports. He has been a member of the Burning Man community since 1999, and he is also involved in the documentation and advancement of “Alternative Culture.” Oroc resides in the Dominican Republic. Jennifer Palmer is a producer of Reality Sandwich, Evolver, and Evolver Intensives—a family of sites dedicated to activating transformational communities both on and offline. She is also a writer, DJ, and philosopher who goes by the name TRUE out on the internets. Her piece about interdependence, telepathy, and Twitter was featured in the anthology Toward 2012: Perspectives on the Next Age . Her blog BRANDTRUEBOY.com started as an art experiment in 2002, in which she posted as three fictitious characters that she passed off as “real” people who emailed, commented, and chatted with other bloggers. Her current projects include working to build the (r)evolution; writing a book about online telepathy, social media and spirituality; honing her Twitter battle rhyme skills; and being blissfully entangled in beautiful webs of synchronicity and magic. Anthony Peake grew up on the Wirral near Liverpool, England. He was educated at Wirral Grammar School, Warwick University, and the London School of Economics. He is a member of the Society for the Study of Dreams, The Scientific & Medical Network, and the Society for Psychic Research. His books include The Out-of-Body Experience: The History and Science of Astral Travel, The Daemon: A Guide to Your Extraordinary Self , and The Labyrinth of Time: Revealing the True Nature of Reality . In 1992 Simon G. Powell suffered an intense bout of psilocybin mushroom fever which led to chronic biophilia and eco-shamanic palsy. He never recovered and has been attempting to delineate the paradigm of natural intelligence ever since. His first book, The Psilocybin Solution , was published by Inner Traditions in July 2011. A second book, Darwin’s Unfinished Business , has just been published (January 2012). Dean Radin is senior scientist at the Institute of Noetic Sciences. His early career as a concert violinist diverted into science after he earned a masters degree in electrical engineering and a PhD in psychology from the University of Illinois, Champaign-Urbana. For a decade he worked on advanced telecommunications R&D at AT&T Bell Laboratories and GTE Laboratories; for over two decades he has been engaged in consciousness research at Princeton University, University of Edinburgh, University of Nevada, and three Silicon Valley think tanks, including SRI International, where he worked on a classified program investigating psychic phenomena for the U.S. government. He is author or coauthor of over two hundred technical and popular articles, a dozen book chapters, and several books including the bestselling The Conscious Universe (HarperOne 1997) and Entangled Minds (Simon & Schuster 2006). His technical articles have appeared in journals ranging from Physics Essays and Foundations of Physics to Psychological Bulletin and Journal of Consciousness Studies . He has been interviewed on television shows ranging from Oprah and Larry King Live to the BBC’s Horizon and PBS’s Closer to Truth, and he has presented over two hundred invited lectures in venues ranging from the physics department at Cambridge University, to the psychology department at Princeton University, the medical school at Stanford University, the Naval War College, and Google. Diana Reed Slattery is a practicing writer, VJ, and xenolinguist. She is currently working on her PhD with the Planetary Collegium on the topic of “Communicating the Unspeakable: Linguistic Phenomena in the Psychedelic Sphere.” She is the author of a novel, The Maze Game . Graham St John is a cultural anthropologist who researches electronic dance music cultures, festivals, and movements. His forthcoming book Global Tribe: Spirituality, Technology and Psytrance is out with Equinox in 2012. His other books include Technomad: Global Raving Countercultures (Equinox 2009), The Local Scenes and Global Culture of Psytrance (Routledge 2010), and FreeNRG: Notes From the Edge of the Dance Floor (Common Ground 2001). He is the executive editor of Dancecult: Journal of Electronic Dance Music Culture ( www.dj.dancecult.net ). His website is at www.edgecentral.net and his blog at www.edgecentral.blogspot.com . Daenin Tajeda currently lives in Seattle. He teaches people how to become energy aware and how to wake up their unique gifts. He is a healer and teacher with the Modern Mystery School in the Lineage of King Solomon. He is into high-vibration nutrition and is currently studying at the Institute for Integrative Nutrition to become a health counselor. He looks for cocreators and collaborators to form community-based action teams of conscious humanitarians. His agenda: share gifts and raise consciousness so we can create new solutions to old problems. Russell Targ , born in Chicago and raised in New York City, is a physicist and author who was a pioneer in the development of the laser and laser applications, and was co-founder of the Stanford Research Institute’s investigation into psychic abilities in the 1970s and 1980s. His work in this new area, called remote viewing, was published in Nature, The Proceedings of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE), and the Proceedings of the American Association for the Advancement of Science . Targ has a BS in Math and Physics from Queens College and did graduate work in physics at Columbia University. He is the coauthor of seven books dealing with the scientific investigation of psychic abilities. Targ has a brand-new book called The Reality of ESP: A Physicist’s Proof of Psychic Abilities , and his website is www.espresearch.com . Michael Taussig is an anthropologist known for his provocative ethnographic studies and unconventional style as an academic. He was born in 1940 in Australia, where he studied medicine at the University of Sydney. He is currently a professor of anthropology at Columbia University in New York. In spite of his numerous publications in his field, especially in medical anthropology, he is most acclaimed for his commentaries on Karl Marx and Walter Benjamin, especially in relation to the idea of commodity fetishism. He is the author of the following books: What Color is the Sacred? (2009), Walter Benjamin’s Grave (2006), My Cocaine Museum (2004), Law in a Lawless Land: Diary of a Limpieza in a Colombian Town (2003), Defacement (1999), Magic of the State (1997), Mimesis and Alterity: A Particular History of the Senses (1993), The Nervous System (1992), Shamanism, Colonialism, and the Wild Man: A Study in Terror and Healing (1987), and The Devil and Commodity Fetishism in South America (1980). Tony Vigorito holds a doctorate in social theory and is the author of the novels Just a Couple of Days and Nine Kinds of Naked . He is currently finishing his third novel. Check out www.tonyvigorito.com . Alberto Villoldo , the author of numerous best-selling books, is a psychologist and medical anthropologist who has studied the spiritual practices of the Amazon and the Andes for more than twenty-five years. While at San Francisco State University, he founded the Biological Self-Regulation Laboratory to study how the mind creates psychosomatic health and disease. Dr. Villoldo directs The Four Winds Society, where he instructs individuals throughout the world in the practice of energy medicine and soul retrieval. He has training centers in New England; California; the UK; the Netherlands; and Park City, Utah. An avid skier, hiker, and mountaineer, he leads annual expeditions to the Amazon and the Andes to work with the wisdom teachers of the Americas. Robert Waggoner is author of the award-winning Lucid Dreaming: Gateway to the Inner Self . An experienced lucid dreamer with approximately one thousand lucid dreams, he coedits the online quarterly e-zine The Lucid Dream Exchange . Waggoner is a past president of the International Association for the Study of Dreams. Timothy Wyllie was born in London in 1940, educated in England, and qualified as an architect before moving to America in 1967. In 1973 he had a full-blown Near Death Experience in which he saw and heard angels, experienced his two companion angels, and was healed and returned to life. He is the author of numerous books, including Dolphins, ETs & Angels , which was published first in 1984 and is still in print. His book Adventures Among Spiritual Intelligences continues the author’s study of angels, extraterrestrials, dolphins, and shamanism; the third book in this series, The Return of the Rebel Angels , was published in October 2011. The Helianx Proposition, Or, The Return of the Rainbow Serpent , published by Daynal Institute Press in 2012, is an illustrated cosmic creation myth that explores the deeper symbolic resonance of the Serpent in the Garden of Eden legend. The first volume of his latest book, The Confessions of a Rebel Angel , will be published by Inner Traditions/Bear & Co. in 2013. Wyllie has lived in the high desert of New Mexico since 1990.
